questions.question: A sentence is given with a blank to be filled in with an appropriate word. Four alternatives are suggested. Choose the correct option. You must _______ for this mistake.
questions.explanation: We need a noun in the given blank, while option C and D are verb. 'courage' does not fit the context of the sentence. 'Account' for means to give a satisfactory record of something or to possess the responsibility of something. Option (a) is the right answer.

questions.question: A sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active voice He could not use the machine because they were servicing it.
options: The machine cannot be used as it was serviced by them
The machine is serviced by them
He cannot use the machine they service
The machine could not be used by him because it was being serviced.
questions.explanation: The given sentence is in active voice. We have to convert it to passive voice. Subject- they, direct object- the machine, indirect object-He, verb- use, tense- past continuous (were servicing). The passive voice of the sentence in past continuous will be in the form: Object + could not+be + past participle of verb + by indirect object Only option 'd' confirms to the above form.

questions.question: A sentence is given with a blank to be filled in with an appropriate word. Four alternatives are suggested. Choose the correct option. The juice of gooseberry is said to be ________.
questions.explanation: Salubrious: healthy. Lugubrious: Sad Savoury: Taste of salt or spicy. We need an adjective in the blank, while C is an adverb. Gooseberry (fruit) juice cannot be salty or spicy. But it is healthy. So, salubrious is the correct option.

questions.question: he asked ________ to go to sea
questions.explanation: Sanction - official grant for something. A will not fit in the context, as the passage is not talking about something formal or official. B is a verb while we need a noun. Between the remaining two words, only C makes contextual sense.

questions.question: he was __________ of the sea
questions.explanation: Fond - to like something. Familiar - to know well. Offended - insulted. B and C will be incorrect as they should be followed by the preposition 'with' instead of 'of'. D makes no contextual sense in the passage. A is the right answer.

questions.question: one of his ___________ stole away
questions.explanation: Comrade - companion/ friend. Possession - something which one owns. We need the plural form of the noun here, as warranted by the construction 'one + of + the + noun'. So, A is incorrect. Also, we need a noun here, so C is incorrect as it is an adjective. D is incorrect as it makes no contextual sense. B is the right answer.

questions.question: a thick fog _________ those
questions.explanation: Hinder - prevent. Obstacle - barrier. Assault - attack. We need a verb here, so C is incorrect as it is a noun. Also, we need the simple past tense of the verb, as the sentence is in past tense. So, B is incorrect as it is in the simple present tense. D makes no contextual sense. A is the right answer.

questions.question: The captain became __________,
questions.explanation: Deceived - tricked. Frightening - causing someone to get scared. Alarmed - scared. We need an adjective here, so C is incorrect as it can act only as a noun or a verb. B is incorrect as we need an adjective that means scared, not one that means causing one to become scared. A makes no contextual sense. D is the right answer.

questions.question: Improve the bracketed part of the sentence. Why can't you just be done with what (he has been trying) to tell you since so long?
questions.explanation: The given sentence is perfectly correct, making option D, the correct answer.

questions.question: Improve the bracketed part of the sentence. Even after all the fuzz, the companies (have still not reach) a consensus.
questions.explanation: The context is of past tense which will require the past tense form of "reach", thereby eliminating the original phrase. Option A houses an incorrectly structured phrase as the infinitive 'to' must be followed by the base form of the verb. "Till" from option B will provide a different meaning, which won't be correct here. Hence, option C will be the correct answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. Now, researchers are working on a new alcohol-monitoring implant that could help people stay on the wagon. Q. All they'll have to give up is some of their autonomy. R. Wherever humans socialize, you can bet that the booze will follow. S. Its omnipresence, plus its addictive qualities, can make it really hard for people to stop drinking, even if they really want to.
questions.explanation: R begins by telling us about the topic of discussion - alcohol. S comments on its omnipresence and tells us that it is hard for people to stop drinking. PQ follow as a pair: P tells us on a scientific method that can help people to stop drinking. Q gives us the cost of using such a method. RSPQ is the final order.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. Firing into the camp, they killed two of the Indians and frightened the rest away. Q. When the girls did not return to their home, Boone knew at once what had happened. R. With some neighbours he started in pursuit. S. Guided by the twigs and bits of cloth they overtook the savages just as they were cooking supper.
questions.explanation: Q begins the passage by introducing the main character - Boone. It tells us that the girls hadn't returned home. R follows by telling us that he started a pursuit accompanied by some neighbours. S continues by telling us how they were guided in their pursuit. P concludes the passage by telling us how they stormed into the camp and killed some Indians. QRSP is the final order.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the word similar in meaning to the word given. Diffident
questions.explanation: 'Diffident' means modest or shy because of a lack of self-confidence. 'Bashful' means reluctant to draw attention to oneself; shy. Clearly, the two words are synonyms of each other, making option B, the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the word similar in meaning to the word given. Culminate
questions.explanation: 'Culminate' means to reach a 'climax' or point of highest development. Hence, option B will be the correct answer. 'Jeopardize' means to put (someone or something) into a situation in which there is a danger of loss, harm, or failure.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given. Ceremonious
questions.explanation: 'Ceremonious' means relating or appropriate to grand and formal occasions. The opposite of this would be unceremonious, meaning having or showing a lack of courtesy; rough or 'abrupt'. Hence, option A will be the correct answer. 'Summation' means summary.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given. Dunce
questions.explanation: 'Dunce' means a person who is slow at learning; a stupid person. 'Genius' will be the apt antonym of it. Hence, option B will be the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/Phrase. A litmus test
options: To know the potency of something
To figure out the viability of something
A method that helps to know if something is correct
To measure the effectiveness of something
questions.explanation: The idiom "A litmus Test" refers to a method employed to determine whether something is correct or not.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/Phrase. Bite off more than you can chew
options: Not able to complete a task due to lack of ability
questions.explanation: The idiom "Bite off more than you can chew" refers to not being able to complete a task due to lack of ability.

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentences and mark it. Transparent and clear
questions.explanation: Lucent means "glowing with or giving off light". "Livid" means "extremely angry". "Loiter" means "to stand or wait around without apparent purpose". Limpid means "clear and transparent"

questions.question: out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentences. A group of people hired to applaud or abuse a performer or public speaker
questions.explanation: Mob: a large crowd of people, especially one that is disorderly and intent on causing trouble or violence. Claque: A group of people hired to applaud or abuse a performer or public speaker. Throng: a large, densely packed crowd of people or animals. Posse: a group of people who have come together for the same purpose

questions.question: Directions for Question: Four words are given in which only one is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: Battalion means a large organised group of people moving with a common aim. There are three syllables in battalion: bat-tal-ion. Example: A battalion of soldiers surrounded the building where terrorists camped. Correct spellings of other options are: Beckon, Banter and Boast

questions.question: Change the form from Direct to Indirect Speech or vice-versa The officer said to the journalists, "Don't enter the premises without written consent."
options: The officer told the journalists not to enter the premises without written consent.
The officer ordered the journalists not to enter the premises without written consent.
The officer asked the journalists not to enter the premises without written consent.
The officer told the journalists to not enter the premises without written consent.
questions.explanation: The statement is an order given by the officer to the journalists. "Ordered" is the best alternative and hence b is the correct answer.

questions.question: Four words are given in which only one is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: Raconteur means storyteller. There are three syllables in raconteur: rac-on-teur. Correct spellings of other options are: Reprimand, Refulgent, Resurrect.

questions.question: Read each sentence to find out whether there is any grammatical error in it. The error, if any, will be in one part of the sentence. The option against that part will be the answer. In case of 'no error', choose option (d) as the answer. (Ignore errors of punctuation, if any.)
options: a whole new suite of claims
questions.explanation: The word 'when' is used with the conjunctive adverb 'hardly' instead of 'than'. 'Than' is usually used when comparing, which is not the case here. Hence, the correct form will be 'had hardly died down when'.

questions.question: In the following questions, the parts of sentence is given in the options. Some part may contain grammatical error. If any part contains error then mark that option as the answer and if there is no error then mark "no error" as the answer.
options: As per the directions of our new editor,
each of the mistakes have to be corrected
before the final version is sent for printing.
questions.explanation: Syntax: Each + of + noun (plural) + verb (singular). Each uses singular verb/auxiliary, hence replace 'have' with 'has'.
